title: schizachyrium scoparium subvar. gracile
intro: Schizachyrium scoparium subvar. gracile, also known as Little Bluestem, is a subvariety of the Little Bluestem grass, a perennial plant native to North America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its roots, which are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Little Bluestem include its ability to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and aid in the treatment of various respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the roots of Little Bluestem can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, or used in salves and creams for topical application, although further research is needed to confirm its full range of uses and potential interactions.
